name: Cinnamon Raisin
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Active Dry Yeast (or 2 tablespoons Active Dry Yeast)
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir together 5 1/2 cups of flour and the yeast in a large mixing bowl.
2. Heat milk, water, 1/4 cup sugar, oil, and salt in a saucepan over very low heat until just warm (120-130°F).
3. Pour the liquid mixture into the flour-yeast mixture. Beat with an electric mixer at medium speed until smooth, about 3 minutes.
4. Blend in the egg, then stir in the raisins.
5. Gradually add enough of the remaining flour to form a moderately soft dough (it will be slightly sticky and light in weight).
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface, adding more flour if needed while kneading, until smooth and satiny, about 10 minutes.
7. Cover with a bowl and let rest on a warm surface for 20 minutes.
8. Combine 1 cup of sugar and cinnamon; set aside.
9. Punch down the dough and divide into two equal pieces.
10. Roll out one piece on a floured surface to a 14 x 7-inch rectangle.
11. Brush with melted butter and sprinkle with half of the cinnamon-sugar mixture.
12. Beginning at the narrow end, roll tightly into a jelly roll shape.
13. Press edges to seal and fold ends under.
14. Place in a greased 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2-inch loaf pan. Repeat with the second piece.
15. Brush tops of loaves with remaining butter.
16. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 45 minutes.
17. Preheat oven to 375°F.
18. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until hollow when tapped.
19. Remove from pans immediately and cool on wire racks.
